applyMediaSettingsOnStart: Apply snapshot media profile on adapter start
apply_overlay_help: Applies every filled-in field of the table below to the matching cameras immediately (no save/restart needed) — both the ioBroker datapoints and MotionEye. Empty fields are left unchanged.
apply_overlay_result_applied: Overlay settings applied. See the adapter log for details.
apply_overlay_result_none: Nothing applied — either the table is empty or none of the rows match a currently running camera (add the camera on the Cameras tab first, save, and restart the instance).
camera_id_help: Stable key for webhooks (e.g. auffahrt). Leave empty to derive from name.
camera_leftText_help: Leave at "unchanged" to skip this field
camera_mediaFolder_help: Folder name under /var/lib/motioneye (e.g. Bambu). Applied on adapter start when MotionEye config sync is enabled. Leave empty to keep the MotionEye default (Camera<ID>). Does not rename existing folders on disk.
camera_name_help: ioBroker channel folder name (lowercase) — independent of MotionEye label
camera_overlayEnabled_help: Leave at "unchanged" to skip this field
camera_rightText_help: Leave at "unchanged" to skip this field
cameras_info: Add each camera you want in ioBroker. The MotionEye ID is shown in the MotionEye web UI or via /config/list on port 8765. After saving, restart the adapter instance to create datapoints and write webhook URLs to MotionEye.
debugging_verbose_hint:
When enabled, the adapter writes significantly more detail to the ioBroker logs:
• MotionEye API requests and HTTP status (paths only, no password)
• Connection and auth diagnostics (password: set/not set and length only)
• Status poll results and motion webhooks

Useful for troubleshooting (e.g. unauthorized) or when reporting issues.
Not recommended for normal operation — log files can grow quickly.
disableStreamOnStart: Disable video stream on adapter start
help_docs:
For complete documentation and troubleshooting (Docker, unauthorized, VIS stream), see:

• Adapter documentation
• FAQ / troubleshooting
help_outro: Team inventwo • https://github.com/inventwo • by skvarel
help_quickstart:
This adapter connects MotionEye cameras to ioBroker.

• Configure connection under Settings (MotionEye host, credentials, webhook host).
• Add cameras under Cameras, save and restart the instance.
• Check _info.connection in the object tree.
• Live stream in VIS: HTML widget with binding to {motioneye.0.<channel>.streamUrl}.
load_cameras_help: Fetches /config/list from MotionEye and adds cameras not yet in the table. The adapter instance must be running. Uses the saved MotionEye password from instance settings. Save configuration and restart after adding cameras.
load_cameras_result_added: New cameras were added to the table. Save the configuration and restart the adapter instance.
load_cameras_result_none: No new cameras — all MotionEye cameras are already in the table.
motionEyePassword_help: Plain-text password as used for MotionEye web login. Leave empty if no password is set.
motionHost_help: IP address or hostname of your MotionEye server
motionResetMs_help: How long the .motion state stays true after a webhook (default 15000)
overlay_info:
Preset text overlay values for cameras from the Cameras tab — one row per camera. Two ways this table takes effect:
• New camera: filled-in fields become the initial value the very first time the camera's overlay datapoints are created.
• Existing camera: filled-in fields are only applied when you click "Apply overlay settings now" below.

Empty fields always mean "leave unchanged" — they never overwrite an existing value. This table is one-way: it never reads back changes you make later via the overlay.* datapoints, so it won't unexpectedly revert live changes on an adapter restart.
test_connection_help: Checks host, port, username, and saved password against the MotionEye config API (/config/list). The adapter instance must be running. On success, camera count is written to the adapter log.
test_connection_result_success: MotionEye API reachable and authenticated. See adapter log for camera count and version.
test_connection_result_unauthorized: Login rejected (unauthorized). Clear the password field, save, re-type the password manually, save again. Confirm web login at http://<host>:8765/ with the same credentials.
streamAutoOffMs_help: Used by streamPulse only — stream turns off after this duration (0 = never)
streamReadyTimeoutMs_help: How long to wait for the MJPEG stream to become reachable after enabling it (expert)
streamRetryMs_help: Delay between stream reachability checks while waiting for the stream to start (expert)
streamSiblingRelinkTimeoutMs_help: How long to retry relinking a stream when another camera on the same device is active (expert, 0 = skip)
streamStartDelayMs_help: Initial wait after enabling the MotionEye video stream before checking reachability (expert)
useMotionEyeConfig_help: Leave enabled for normal use. The adapter writes camera mode, webhook URLs, and stream on/off to MotionEye (port 8765). ioBroker then controls motion detection and live streams; the MotionEye web UI follows as a side effect. Disable only if you configure MotionEye entirely by hand and ioBroker should only receive webhooks.
webhookBind_help: Network interface for the built-in webhook listener (default 0.0.0.0)
webhookHost_help: IP or hostname of your ioBroker host reachable from MotionEye (e.g. 192.168.130.130). Required — without this value the adapter cannot write webhook URLs to MotionEye.
webhookPort_help: Port for motion webhooks from MotionEye (default 8090). Ensure firewall allows inbound traffic.
